description_long: This object is a large, modern solar energy infrastructure installation, likely designed for urban or commercial use. It features a prominent array of rectangular solar panels mounted on a robust, flat platform with a geometric, stepped base. The panels are arranged in a grid and are elevated above the main structure, which appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and glass-like materials. The base includes patterned surfaces and raised sections, possibly for structural support or integrated utility access. There are also several smaller components and enclosures, which may house electrical or monitoring equipment. The overall design is sleek and industrial, suitable for integration into rooftops or dedicated solar farms in urban environments.
description: A large, sleek solar energy installation with a grid of elevated panels on a geometric, stepped base, featuring metallic and glass-like materials and integrated utility enclosures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grid of solar panels elevated above a patterned, stepped base with visible enclosures and geometric detailing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the elongated, flat platform with raised and recessed sections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panel array and the full extent of the patterned base, with utility enclosures visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the length of the structure, the tilt of the panels, and the stepped, patterned surface.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted or platform-mounted with visible aluminum or steel frame
attribute_array_configuration: single grid of approximately 3x4 panels, closely spaced in a rectangular arrangement
